definition: AGGREGATOR identifies the AS and BGP speaker that performed route aggregation when the attribute is present.
An announcement is BGP information indicating reachability for a prefix through a path and attributes.

An AS is an independently managed network or group of networks that participates in inter-domain routing under a common routing policy.
AS relationship describes inferred or known business and routing relationships such as provider-customer and peer-peer links between ASes.
Route leak conclusions can be wrong when AS relationship data is inaccurate, outdated, or too coarse for prefix/location-specific policy.
AS_PATH is the ordered path attribute listing ASNs that a BGP route announcement has traversed.
Ordered sequence of ASNs in the route announcement.
AS_PATH prepending repeats an ASN in the path to influence route selection by making a path appear longer.
Sequence of provider-customer or peer-peer relationships along an AS_PATH.
An ASN is the numeric identifier assigned to an autonomous system and used in BGP paths and policy.
Autonomous System Provider Authorization records allow an AS holder to authorize upstream provider ASNs and support BGP path verification and route-leak prevention work.
ASPA-based path verification uses RPKI ASPA objects that authorize upstream providers to evaluate whether AS paths are plausible with respect to provider relationships.
ASRank rank is a topology-derived ranking value for an AS in CAIDA ASRank outputs.
Relationship field describes inferred AS link relationship in ASRank or CAIDA relationship data.
ATOMIC_AGGREGATE indicates that route aggregation occurred and some AS path detail may have been lost.

term: BEAR: BGP Event Analysis and Reporting
definition: BGP anomaly event explanation
A BGP event can be explained by comparing historical, pre-event, and post-event AS paths for affected prefixes.
BGP is the inter-domain routing protocol used by autonomous systems to exchange reachability information for IP prefixes.
The BGP decision process selects routes from Adj-RIBs-In into the Loc-RIB and organizes selected routes for advertisement through Adj-RIBs-Out.
BGP conceptually separates learned routes, selected local routes, and advertised routes into Adj-RIBs-In, Loc-RIB, and Adj-RIBs-Out.

term: BGP Roles and OTC Route Leak Prevention
definition: RFC9234 uses configured BGP Roles and the Only to Customer path attribute to prevent and detect certain route leaks during ingress and egress processing.
A BGP session is a peering relationship between BGP speakers over which routing information is exchanged.
A BGP speaker is a router or process that participates in BGP and exchanges routing information with peers.
A BGP Update message announces new route attributes or withdraws previously announced reachability.
BGP UPDATE messages announce feasible routes with path attributes and NLRI or withdraw previously advertised routes from service.
BGP Identifier is a four-octet identifier included in the OPEN message to identify a BGP speaker.
BGP Role characterizes the configured relationship between eBGP speakers for route leak prevention and detection procedures.
BGPsec extends BGP with path security mechanisms intended to protect the AS path in UPDATE messages using BGPsec path attributes and router certificates.
BGP anomaly detection using AS semantic descriptions
BGPStream is an open-source framework for accessing and processing live and historical BGP measurement data.
BGPStream is an open-source framework for accessing and processing live and historical BGP data from multiple underlying sources.
CAIDA AS Relationships provides inferred AS business relationships and customer-cone related topology data for Internet topology and routing analysis.
CAIDA ASRank provides API access to AS ranking, organization, degree, customer cone, and AS-link relationship data derived from Internet topology measurements.
case_celerbridge_bgp_hijack
Collector name or ID where the BGP observation was made.
A customer cone is the set of ASes, prefixes, or addresses reachable from an AS by following customer links in an AS relationship graph.
Customer cone ASNs describe the ASes reachable from an AS through customer links in a topology-derived relationship graph.
eBGP is BGP used between different autonomous systems.
case_facebook_2021_outage
A FIB is the forwarding table used by a router to forward packets after route selection.
Hold Time is negotiated in the OPEN message and controls the maximum time between received KEEPALIVE or UPDATE messages before the session is considered failed.
iBGP is BGP used within a single autonomous system to distribute BGP-learned routes internally.
case_indosat_route_leak
IRR data contains routing policy and route object records that can support prefix and origin-AS interpretation.
Semantics-aware BGP routing anomaly detection
Multiple origin ASes can be legitimate because of anycast, multi-homing, managed services, or operational transition.
LOCAL_PREF is a BGP path attribute used inside an AS to express route preference, where higher preference is selected before lower preference under the decision process.
MULTI_EXIT_DISC is an optional path attribute used to convey preference among multiple entry points into a neighboring AS.
MOAS occurs when multiple ASes originate the same prefix. It can be benign or anomalous depending on authorization and operational context.
MOAS describes a condition where more than one AS originates the same prefix. It can be benign or anomalous depending on authorization, anycast, multi-homing, and operational context.
MRT is a common format used to store BGP routing table snapshots and update data from collectors.
MRT file type indicates whether a file is a routing table dump/snapshot or an update stream interval.
NEXT_HOP identifies the next hop IP address used for the route described by a BGP UPDATE.
Network Layer Reachability Information lists prefixes advertised as reachable in a BGP UPDATE message.
The origin AS is the AS that originates reachability for a prefix and is usually represented by the rightmost AS in a simple AS_PATH.
Origin change is an observed change in the AS originating a prefix; it is a signal that requires authorization and context checks before labeling as an attack.
AS inferred or recorded as originating the prefix.
The ORIGIN path attribute indicates the origin type of the route information.
Only to Customer is an optional transitive BGP path attribute used to mark routes that should subsequently go only to customers.
case_pakistan_youtube_2008
A path hijack or path manipulation event involves suspicious alteration of AS_PATH information while the origin may or may not change.
Path manipulation refers to suspicious AS_PATH changes such as forged, removed, or misleading AS segments.
BGP propagates reachability with AS-level path information so receiving ASes can reason about loops and policy.
Path Attributes carry route attributes such as ORIGIN, AS_PATH, NEXT_HOP, LOCAL_PREF, and others for prefixes in the UPDATE.
A peer is a BGP neighbor that exchanges routes with another BGP speaker or collector.
ASN of the peer that provides BGP routes to a collector.
PeeringDB provides network, facility, exchange, and interconnection metadata that can help interpret where and how networks peer.
A prefix is an IP address block announced and routed as a unit, usually represented in CIDR notation.
IP prefix whose reachability or authorization is being described.
A prefix hijack occurs when an AS illegitimately announces reachability for a prefix it does not own or is not authorized to originate.
A prefix outage occurs when reachability for a prefix disappears or sharply degrades across measurement vantage points.
A RIB is a routing information table containing candidate or selected routes known to a routing process or collector snapshot.
RIPE RIS is a RIPE NCC BGP routing data collection platform using route collectors and peers.
RIPE RIS is a BGP routing data collection platform with Route Collectors, raw MRT data, and peer metadata.
RIPEstat Data API exposes RIPE NCC Internet resource data, including endpoints relevant to BGP visibility, prefix/origin context, RPKI validation, and routing history.
A ROA authorizes an AS to originate a prefix, optionally with a maximum prefix length.
Route origin validation result for a prefix-origin pair under available ROA data.
A route collector receives BGP routes from peers and archives routing information for measurement and analysis.
A route leak occurs when routes are propagated beyond their intended routing-policy scope, often violating expected provider-customer or peer-peer export behavior.
Route Origin Validation checks whether a prefix-origin AS pair is valid, invalid, or not-found under available ROA data.
RouteViews is a BGP measurement project that collects routing information from global peers and exposes BGP data and APIs.
RouteViews collects BGP routing information from global peers and exposes collector, peer, RIB, prefix, ASN, and RPKI-related API endpoints.
RouteViews API endpoint identifies which RouteViews API resource was queried, such as collectors, peers, ASN, prefix, RIB, or RPKI endpoints.
ROV validates whether a BGP route's prefix-origin pair is authorized by available RPKI data.
RPKI is an infrastructure for cryptographically attesting Internet number resource holder information and enabling route origin validation.
RPKI ROA data provides cryptographic statements that authorize an AS to originate a prefix within an allowed prefix length.
The RPKI-to-Router protocol delivers validated RPKI prefix-origin data and router keys from a cache to routers for validation use.
RPKI-to-Router PDUs carry validated RPKI cache information such as prefix origin data and router keys to routers.
RRC identifies a RIPE RIS route collector whose peers contribute BGP data to RIS raw data files and services.
Short-lived route changes or withdrawals may be normal instability rather than an outage or attack.
An anomaly seen from one collector or a small peer set may reflect local visibility, policy, or failure rather than a global event.
A subprefix hijack occurs when an AS announces a more-specific prefix inside a victim prefix, attracting traffic for that subrange by longest-prefix matching.
Time when the observation, snapshot, or event occurred.
Whether a BGP update announces or withdraws reachability.
Valley-free routing describes policy-compliant AS path propagation constrained by provider-customer and peer-peer relationships.
Valley-free routing models expected AS path export behavior using provider-customer and peer-peer relationships.
A vantage point is a measurement position from which BGP reachability or path information is observed.
case_vodafone_2021_route_leak
VRP ASN is the origin AS number authorized in a Validated ROA Payload.
VRP max length is the maximum prefix length authorized by a ROA-derived validated payload.
VRP prefix is the IP prefix component of a Validated ROA Payload used in route origin validation.
WHOIS and RDAP provide registration information that can support resource ownership and contact checks.
A withdrawal removes previously announced reachability for one or more prefixes.
Withdrawn Routes lists prefixes being removed from service in a BGP UPDATE message.
